category_title: Pupils who act out due to frustration with learning tasks
scenario_title: Frustration and acting out during difficult independent work
minimal_context: The student is completing an independent test at their desk in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please start working on your test now. You need to complete this independently.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the paper) I don't really like the work, it's too hard.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I've gubbed up [made a mess of it] already. Doing a test or something, I probably know I could have done better.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Try to figure it out on your own.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Pushes the test away) I'll just carry on and then get into trouble. I get frustrated when I don't get help when I need it!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please start working on your test now. You need to complete this independently.

STUDENT: (Looks at the paper) I don't really like the work, it's too hard.

TUTOR: Just keep working on it.

STUDENT: I've gubbed up [made a mess of it] already. Doing a test or something, I probably know I could have done better.

TUTOR: Try to figure it out on your own.

STUDENT: (Pushes the test away) I'll just carry on and then get into trouble. I get frustrated when I don't get help when I need it!
